column3: Reporting to the Head of Global Reporting, Accounting and Regulatory Reporting Manager, Canada will play a pivotal role in overseeing and managing portions of Coinbase Global’s operational finance and accounting tasks as well as ensuring adherence to the regulatory framework outlined by the Canadian Securities Administration (CSA) for Crypto Trading Platforms (CTPs) and National Instrument 31-103 and the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO). This position will directly own the maintenance of the books of account under IFRS and will also include involvement in the close process including preparing and/or reviewing internal and external financial reporting and analyses. The role will require collaboration with various teams across the organization to ensure the complete and accurate delivery of reporting to&amp

column9: The Analytics Engineering team builds and maintains trusted data infrastructure across the organization. This role focuses on building reliable, well-governed data foundations for compliance and regulatory reporting across Coinbase&#39

column3: As Coinbase&#39
column4: s international presence grows, so does the volume and complexity of regulatory data requirements. Your mission is to build a platform: a reliable, automated, self-serve data foundation for regulatory reporting. column9: The U.S. Withholding Tax Associate Manager will be an integral part of the Coinbase tax team, supporting the day-to-day operations of the U.S. withholding tax function, including backup withholding and nonresident alien (NRA) withholding. This role will focus on ensuring Coinbase&#39
column10: s compliance with U.S. withholding tax requirements and collaborate cross-functionally with product, platform, and data teams to drive continuous process improvements.&lt

column3: The Coinbase tax team ensures full compliance with tax obligations across all jurisdictions in which Coinbase operates, including building world-class tax protocols for digital asset transactions. We are passionate about tax and crypto, and we are seeking a candidate with a similar passion and collaborative mindset to join our team.&lt
&lt
Ensure accurate application of backup withholding and NRA withholding rates on reportable transactions&lt
Monitor and ensure timely withholding tax deposits in accordance with IRS requirements&lt
Reconcile withholding tax liabilities and support preparation of Form 945 and Form 1042&lt
Monitor withholding rate changes and coordinate system updates accordingly&lt
Track and respond to IRS notices including B-notices, C-notices, and withholding tax assessments&lt
Support withholding tax audits and tax authority requests&lt
Research and analyze U.S. withholding tax developments and assess impact on Coinbase operations&lt
Identify and implement improvements to withholding tax processes and procedures&lt
Respond to inquiries from product, onboarding, and customer support teams regarding U.S. withholding tax requirements&lt
Partner with product and data teams on system requirements and business rules for withholding tax processes&lt
Support customer service teams through internal education and customer escalations related to withholding tax&lt
Coordinate across departments including product, data analytics, onboarding, customer service, and communications&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you:&lt

column3: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or similar field&lt
4-7+ years of experience in U.S. withholding tax, ideally with a bank, broker-dealer, large financial institution or multinational or Big 4&lt
Strong technical knowledge of backup withholding (IRC Section 3406) and NRA withholding (IRC Sections 1441-1446)&lt
Experience with U.S. withholding tax deposits, reconciliations, and filings (Form 945, Form 1042)&lt
Familiarity with IRS notice processes, including B-notices and C-notices&lt
Experience with withholding tax systems and tools&lt
Experience with withholding tax system implementations or process automation&lt
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain withholding tax requirements to both tax and non-tax audiences&lt
Strong attention to detail and process orientation&lt
Collaborative mindset and positive attitude&lt
Willingness to adapt and learn in a fast-paced environment&lt
&lt
Experience with data analytics tools such as Snowflake, Looker, or similar platforms for withholding tax reconciliation and reporting&lt
IRS withholding tax audit or examination experience&lt
Basic knowledge of SQL for data extraction and analysis&lt
Experience with withholding tax system implementations or process automation&lt

column3: Coinbase is looking for a highly motivated Business Controller Intern to work with our Business Controller (BC) team and play a meaningful role in our continued growth and achievement of operational excellence during the internship.&amp
column5: The BC Intern must possess some accounting knowledge, an ability to communicate clearly and work with various teams inside of Finance (such as Tax, Finance Analytics, Platform Accounting, etc.) and with XFN stakeholders (Product, Compliance, Legal, etc), and a desire to learn, work outside of their comfort zone and work through ambiguity in a dynamic, high-growth technology company.&lt

column5: A, Product, Engineering, Compliance, Legal to ensure proper finance impact of product launches have been identified and addressed.&amp

column3: Maintain compliance with internal controls, policies and procedures.&lt

&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Currently pursuing a BA/BS degree in Accounting or related field&lt
Possess excellent analytical skills, business partnering, problem solving and prioritization skills&lt

Basic knowledge and experience using AI tools such as LibreChat, Claude, etc. and building automated workflows and agents.&lt
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal&lt
Basic knowledge Coinbase, digital assets and the crypto economy, or a desire to learn&lt

column5: This client-facing role will include leading go to market for new product launches, close coordination with our product teams, and navigating across Coinbase’s senior leadership to ensure our partnerships are successful.&amp

column3: Ideal candidates have experience selling into large financial institutions and banks. Direct experience in crypto is positive but not required. &lt

&lt

&lt

column9: The Institutional Compliance team supports the Institutional Product Group to develop effective compliance programs to meet Coinbase’s regulatory obligations across the business.&amp
column11: In this role, you will fulfill Coinbase’s mission of being the most trusted cryptocurrency company by overseeing and managing all aspects of Coinbase’s Compliance and Anti-Money Laundering Programs in Bermuda. In addition, you will be responsible for managing the entity’s relationship with the Bermuda Monetary Authority and providing strategic advisory to our business and product teams on all types of Compliance issues. This role will be supported by Coinbase’s global Compliance infrastructure, and report to the Head of Institutional Compliance. &lt
column15: This role is remote and can be based outside of Bermuda (US, UK, or Ireland). The individual will be expected to travel to Bermuda approximately four times per year.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Identify relevant regulatory requirements for Coinbase Bermuda and ensure Coinbase’s ongoing compliance with the same.&amp
Keep ahead of regulatory developments in Bermuda that could impact Coinbase’s business and deploy necessary changes to maintain compliance in partnership with internal stakeholders.&lt

Provide regular updates to Bermuda senior management and the Coinbase Bermuda Board of Directors regarding the Compliance Program and overall regulatory landscape. Ensure appropriate escalation of compliance incidents or material issues to Compliance and business leadership and the Board.&amp
Oversee / manage regulatory notifications, comment letters, and other communications as required. Handle day-to-day regulatory communications.&amp
Oversee execution of Compliance Risk Assessments to ensure the adequacy of systems and controls to ensure that risks are managed effectively.&amp
Ensure that policies are updated to meet changing regulatory and business requirements.&amp
Oversee deployment of relevant Compliance training for local employees&lt
Ensure timely remediation of all relevant findings identified by third-parties (Internal Audit, regulatory exams), as well as self-identified items&lt

Partner with Compliance and Product stakeholders to ensure compliance obligations are considered and built as part of new product or expansion initiatives. Ensure appropriate documentation, including updating policies and procedures as needed.&amp

Manage and develop global relationships with key internal stakeholders and senior management in order to promote a consistent approach to the development and execution of an effective and integrated control environment.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: 8+ years experience in compliance, including in senior leadership roles&lt
Direct, first-hand knowledge of financial services and anti-money laundering regulations in Bermuda, including direct experience liaising with Bermuda Monetary Authority&lt
Ability to independently manage day-to-day aspects of a Compliance Program, including audits and exams.&amp
Experience building Compliance Programs.&amp
Experience in developing and administering internal controls and processes, proven track record of partnership and collaboration with remote, global teams.&lt
Ability to work collaboratively with a broad range of business functions, with an emphasis on senior management.&lt
Demonstrated ability to generate and execute strategies across a global organization.&lt
Ability to develop, foster and maintain effective working relationships with diverse groups of people within the organization as well as with external stakeholders and vendors.&lt
&lt
Prior experience at cryptocurrency or fintech preferred&lt
A professional compliance or finance qualification, and membership of a recognized professional body&lt
Job #: P76062&lt

The Global Complaints team is divided into three units, namely&amp
column5: Consumer Complaints, Regulatory Complaints and Litigation Support. Each unit is made up of L3+ complaints analysts responsible for production work - they are expected to accurately review and respond to complaints within service levels.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Responsible for providing support for Coinbase customers that submitted complaints through different channels by investigating, troubleshooting and responding to complaints. Lead the charge in swiftly gathering and documenting customer issues and resolutions with unparalleled efficiency.&lt

Manage, prioritize, and respond to Tier 2 issues, inquiries, and escalations and resolve complex escalations with minimal guidance, demonstrating your adeptness in leveraging available tools effectively.&lt
Communicate with internal and external stakeholders in an effective, tactical, and empathetic manner.&lt
Set a high bar for support analysts within the organization by setting an example through performance and work ethic.&lt

Take the reins in providing comprehensive support for consumer complaints across all pillars,, spearheading thorough investigations across all channels and take ownership of processes or act as a Subject Matter Expert to analyze and understand existing workflows.&lt
Identify gaps in processes and propose actionable improvements to enhance efficiency.&lt
Proactively lead efforts to streamline operations and boost productivity.&lt
Play a key role in shaping and optimizing internal processes for sustainable growth and success.&lt

Represents the voice of our customer in proactively driving impactful changes across workflows, policies and tools by succinctly relaying customer feedback in escalations to internal support teams and Complaints leadership.&lt

Our &lt

column3: There are a few things we look for across all hires we make at Coinbase, regardless of role or team. First, we look for candidates who will thrive in a&lt
column9: like ours, where we default to trust, embrace feedback, and disrupt ourselves. Second, we expect all employees to commit to our&lt
column15: to our work. Finally, we seek people who are excited to learn about and live crypto, because those are the folks who enjoy the intense moments in our sprint and recharge work culture. We’re a remote-first company looking to hire the absolute best talent all over the world.&lt

column3: Ready to #LiveCrypto? Who you are:&lt
You’ve got positive energy. You’re optimistic about the future and determined to get there.&amp
You’re never tired of learning. You want to be a pro in bleeding edge tech like DeFi, NFTs, DAOs, and Web 3.0.&amp

You appreciate direct communication. You’re both an active communicator and an eager listener - because let’s face it, you can’t have one without the other. You’re cool with candid feedback and see every setback as an opportunity to grow.&lt

You can pivot on the fly. Crypto is constantly evolving, so our priorities do, too. What you worked on last month may not be what you work on today, and that excites you. You’re not looking for a boring job.&lt

You have a “can do” attitude. Our teams create high-quality work on quick timelines. Owning a problem doesn’t scare you, but rather empowers you to take 100% responsibility for achieving our mission.&lt
You want to be part of a winning team. We’re stronger together, and you’re a person who embraces being pushed out of your comfort zone.&lt

column9: The Counsel, Commercial (Institutional Legal) role will be based in the U.S. working as part of the Institutional Legal team covering U.S. markets and professional trading clients including banks, brokers, hedge funds, market makers corporates and other professional traders. The team consists of a small but highly experienced group of lawyers from a combination of traditional finance and fintech backgrounds. The role reports into the head of the team and covers institutional products including prime brokerage, exchange, trading and custody, derivatives and financing products. We’re looking for a lawyer with solid product development, regulatory and negotiation skills preferably developed within the traditional prime brokerage, securities financing, and/or derivatives sectors. A sense of curiosity, flexibility and adventure combined with the desire to make an impact at the intersection of crypto and traditional global markets is essential.&amp

column3: Work as the trusted legal advisor across the Marketing and Growth teams with particular attention provided to bringing brand campaigns to market, sponsorship agreements, advertising and product claim substantiation, and promotions.&lt

Monitor the evolving global regulatory landscape and translate complex international laws into clear, commercially practical guidance, empowering the Marketing team to launch cross-border campaigns with confidence.&amp
Scale the advice you provide, so that you can do more with less and grow efficiently with the business&lt
Operate in a fast paced, demanding environment and pivot quickly to meet leadership&#39
Identify and resolve issues impacting Marketing and Growth that fall outside your immediate scope by partnering with other members of the Coinbase Legal team.&lt
Manage outside counsel effectively where they are engaged in connection with specific projects, while establishing your own strong domain expertise&lt

Experience advising on domestic and international sweepstakes, contests and other promotions and incentives&lt

Manage complex projects involving coordination across multiple time zones and tight deadlines&lt

Willingness to roll up sleeves (no job too big, no job too small)&lt
&lt

column9: Join Coinbase’s Canada Country Pod to own the region’s overall strategy and business performance. Drive cross-functional execution across Product, Policy, and Marketing to launch locally relevant initiatives that accelerate user growth and revenue. Blending executive leadership with regulatory advocacy to build a high-performing team and secure Coinbase’s long-term market position in Canada.&lt

column3: &lt
Own and drive Coinbase’s overall business performance in Canada, with accountability for user growth, revenue, and long-term market position&lt
Define and execute the country strategy, identifying high-impact growth opportunities grounded in market, regulatory, and competitive analysis&lt
Partner with Product, Engineering, and Design to shape and launch locally-relevant products, ensuring strong product-market fit within regulatory constraints&lt
Lead cross-functional execution in partnership with Marketing, Business Development, Legal, Compliance, and Policy to deliver Canada-specific initiatives at speed&lt
Play a senior leadership role in regulatory and policy engagement, helping unlock new products and ensure ongoing compliance with Canadian requirements&lt
Build, hire, and develop a high-performing local team, while reinforcing Coinbase’s mission, values, and operating standards&lt
Serve as a senior representative of Coinbase in Canada, engaging with regulators, key partners, clients, media, and industry forums as appropriate&lt

&lt

column3: Senior leadership experience (CEO, COO, GM, Founder, or equivalent) leading and scaling a fintech business in Canada, with measurable impact on revenue and/or active users&lt
Completion of the Canadian Securities Institute (CSI) – Partners, Directors and Senior Officers Course (PDO) and a minimum of 5 years’ experience in the financial services industry&lt
Deep understanding of the Canadian regulated financial services ecosystem, including experience operating under regulatory oversight&lt
Proven track record partnering with Product, Marketing, and Business Development to build, launch, and scale products in regulated environments&lt
Direct experience engaging with regulators and policy stakeholders, with the ability to balance constructive relationships and commercial outcomes&lt
Strong understanding of how regulated financial institutions operate across functions such as Compliance, Risk, Finance, Treasury, Audit, and third-party oversight&lt
Comfortable operating in a remote-first, globally matrixed organization, aligning local execution with global standards&lt
Highly pragmatic and solutions-oriented, with a bias toward action and continuous improvement&lt
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and external audiences&lt
Structured, analytical problem-solver who can navigate ambiguity and make sound decisions with imperfect information&lt

&lt
Previous experience working in a multinational / US-headquartered business&lt
Experience with retail-oriented payments, trading, or brokerage platforms&lt
Previous work experience or personal interest in crypto/blockchain space&lt

column9: We are looking for a Data Protection Engineer (L4) to help implement and maintain our data protection capabilities, ensuring the Coinbase ecosystem remains resilient against security threats. You will contribute to our data protection strategy, helping to balance strong security controls with the speed of a decentralized tech environment. You will focus on automating data protection operations, utilizing tools including LLMs and agentic AI, to build scalable Data Loss Prevention (DLP) solutions. You will work with other teams to mitigate risk and ensure compliance with global regulations.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Support and expand data loss prevention capabilities to protect Coinbase ecosystem from sophisticated data security incidents&lt
Support execution of&amp
column5: the long term strategy for the Data prevention program to harden security posture against data security threats&lt

column3: Implement and maintain complex designs/controls across a decentralized tech environment to promote security without impeding the speed of business&lt
Implement and deploy Data Protection tools and technologies in concert with cross functional teams (ie. endpoint security, information technology, and others)&lt
Demonstrate operational efficiency across a wide array of DLP Engineering &amp
column5: Operations leveraging LLMs and agentic AI&lt

column3: Bring an automation first mindset to champion and drive a more streamlined and scalable approach towards DLP operations&lt
Produce quantitative and qualitative metrics to apprise Data Protection Leadership of programmatic impact and challenges&lt
Collaborate across Security and Privacy Teams and lead cross functional data protection initiatives&lt
Collaborate with cross functional teams like engineering, product development, compliance to ensure timely remediation&lt
Work in concert with risk teams to measure control effectiveness and address changes in laws/regulations globally&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&amp

column3: You are an experienced security engineer in the data protection space who can deliver measurable results&lt
You have the technical acumen to solve operational issues with an engineering solution&lt
You have direct experience testing, tuning, and implementing data prevention controls across multiple OS stack&lt
You are comfortable manipulating and orchestrating controls to address multiple operating systems (iOS / Chrome) and decentralized datasets&lt
You are adept at creating scalable processes, automating where possible, and leveraging ML/AI where feasible to maximize efficiency&lt
You can be trusted to be discreet and thoughtful while working&amp
You are actively aware of the insider threat landscape, and understand the legal, regulatory, and ethical considerations of working with sensitive data across a global enterprise&amp

You have experience working and building Insider Threat technologies (such as Security Information Event Management - SIEM, User Behavioral Analytics - UBA, Data Loss Prevention - DLP) and an understanding of investigations and/or the intelligence cycle&lt

You have excellent verbal and written communication skills. Other team members ask for your input to communicate clearly and concisely and you are comfortable composing briefs and assessments consumed by leadership&lt
Business acumen: Proven understanding of business dynamics, goals, and product strategy. Knowledge of how Security fits into Coinbase’s overall business.&lt
The ability to balance business needs, a sense of urgency, conflicting constraints, and shipping high quality and pragmatic solutions in a fast-moving and quickly-growing company.&lt

Demonstrates the ability to responsibly use generative AI tools and copilots (e.g., LibreChat, Gemini, Glean) in daily workflows, continuously learn as tools evolve, and apply human‑in‑the‑loop practices to deliver business‑ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, and quality.&lt
&lt
3+ years of security engineering experience solving complex security challenges across enterprise-wide DLP Programs&amp
Solving operational problems leveraging engineering / automation first mindset&lt
Experience in Web3 and crypto forward organizations or traditional financial institutions&lt

column9: Data Science is an integral component of Coinbase’s product and decision making process: we work in partnership with Product, Engineering and Design to influence the roadmap and better understand our users. With a deep expertise in experimentation, analytics and advanced modeling, we produce insights which directly move the company’s bottom line.&lt

column3: We’re looking for a Data Scientist II to join Coinbase’s Platform Data Science team. Platform data scientists partner with product, engineering, and design to improve the core systems and user journeys that power Coinbase spanning areas like Payments, Risk &amp
column7: Onboarding, Compliance, Customer Support, and Experimentation.&amp

column3: As a Data Scientist II on Platform, you’ll work on high-impact, cross-functional problems where the goal is to make Coinbase safer, more reliable, and easier to use, while unlocking growth through better decision-making and measurement. Sample projects include diagnosing funnel drop-offs and reliability issues across critical user journeys (e.g., onboarding, payments, authentication), designing north star + guardrail + diagnostic metric systems and running and improving experimentation and causal inference approaches (A/B tests, quasi-experiments, incrementality measurement).&lt
&lt
Perform analyses on products to answer open-ended questions and provide strategic recommendations.&lt
Design and guide experiments/analysis to measure impact and drive product improvements.&lt
Develop and maintain key metrics and reports, enhancing data infrastructure for better analysis.&lt
&lt
column7: What we look for in you:&lt

column3: At least a BA/BS in a quantitative field (ex Math, Stats, Physics, or Computer Science) with ≥2+ years of relevant experience.&lt
Experience driving impact for a digital product with an iterative development cycle.&lt
Understanding of statistical concepts and practical experience applying them (in A|B testing, causal inference, ML, etc.).&amp
Experience in data analyses using SQL.&lt
Experience in programming/modeling in Python.&lt
Demonstration of our core cultural values: clear communication, positive energy, continuous learning, and efficient execution.&lt
&lt

column9: Coinbase is looking for a Director of Operational Risk, reporting to the VP of Financial &amp
column11: Operational Risk, to mature this global second-line function. This role will work cross-functionally with Technology Risk, Enterprise Risk, and product and engineering teams across the company to ensure Coinbase effectively identifies and manages its operational risk and resiliency. This is a high-impact leadership position that ensures we identify and mitigate systemic vulnerabilities while enabling the business to scale safely.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Lead the global Operational Risk function, evolving the second line of defense framework across the entire organization.&lt

Identify and measure operational risks, including via managing the Risk and Control Self-Assessment (RCSA) program, and recommend and prioritize best practice controls to maintain operational risk within appetite&lt
Develop and monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) to provide senior leadership and the Board with data-driven insights into the company’s operational health.&lt
Evolve the operational incident management process. Perform root cause analysis, scenario stress testing, and track remediation efforts to prevent recurrence of systemic failures or losses.&lt
Evaluate the operational readiness and risk profile of new business initiatives.&lt

Oversee Operational Resiliency, including business continuity and crisis management. Embed resiliency into the product lifecycle, ensuring the firm can maintain critical services through disruptions by aligning with global standards and local regulatory requirements.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: 15+ years of operational risk management experience within a regulated financial institution or fintech.&lt
Deep expertise in ORM frameworks and a proven track record of implementing scalable programs.&lt
Collaborative business partner while remaining able to challenge first-line owners effectively.&lt
Clear communicator who can translate complex operational failures into actionable insights for senior stakeholders.&lt
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to navigate ambiguity, operate flexibly in a high-growth environment, and drive effective solutions.&lt
&lt
Crypto industry experience.&lt
Advanced degree or professional risk certifications.&lt

column9: The Coinbase Internal Audit is an independent, third line function that reports directly to the Coinbase Audit Committee. The purpose and mission of Internal Audit is to objectively evaluate and audit the effectiveness of governance, compliance, risk management, and control process. The in-house Coinbase Internal Audit team is responsible for strategic analysis, risk assessments, development of the audit plan, project management, quality review, report issuance and issue validation across IT and business functions. By executing all aspects of audits, including planning, walkthroughs, testing, deficiency remediation, and report writing, you&#39
column10: ll play a key role in strengthening our position as the industry leader by evaluating the effectiveness of management’s ability to control potential events or threats and ultimately add value to all our stakeholders.&lt

column3: &lt
As a part of a global internal audit team and working closely with out-sourced team(s), develop next generation audit practices to maintain a best-in-class global internal audit function&lt

Responsible for supporting Coinbase’s internal audit plan through the execution of operational and compliance related audits from beginning to end. Key activities include audit planning, stakeholder management, fieldwork execution, reporting and validation of remediated audit findings&lt
General Duties:&lt
Support the Operations and Compliance Internal Audit team in executing a risk-based audit plan&lt
Remain current and increase knowledge of regulatory requirements that impact the Operations and Compliance space&lt
Build effective working relationships with first- and second-line stakeholders across Operations, Compliance, Risk, Finance, Legal, and related teams&lt
Manage day-to-day execution tasks in a remote environment, including timely follow-ups, status updates, and documentation&lt

Leverage generative AI tools and copilots (e.g., LibreChat, Gemini, Glean) in daily audit workflows to drive measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, and quality, while applying human-in-the-loop practices to deliver business-ready outputs&lt
Audit Execution Responsibilities:&lt

Support audit planning activities, including understanding business objectives and relevant regulations, scoping, conducting preliminary risk/control assessments, and drafting planning documentation&lt
Perform walkthroughs and document processes/controls with accuracy and completeness—getting the details right matters&lt
Track audit requests, open items, and testing status, keeping stakeholders informed with proactive and transparent updates&lt
Execute control testing (design and operating effectiveness), including defining test steps, selecting samples, requesting evidence, and evaluating results with rigor and a bias for action&lt
Identify control gaps and support development of practical, risk-based recommendations, co-creating solutions with stakeholders rather than defaulting to blame or criticism&lt
Draft clear, complete workpapers that tie testing procedures to evidence and conclusions, holding yourself to a high standard of quality and craft&lt
Support audit reporting by drafting result summaries and contributing to issue write-ups that are direct, concise, and actionable&lt
Support issue follow-up activities, including documenting management responses and action plans, and driving issues through to closure&lt
Validate remediation (MAPs) for prior audit findings by assessing and documenting evidence of implementation and confirming evidence is sufficient for closure&lt
&lt
3+ years of experience in internal audit, external audit, compliance testing, risk management, consulting or related control-focused roles&lt

Experience executing audits end-to-end (planning, testing, and reporting) focused on business processes, operations, and/or compliance within financial services, fintech, payments, brokerage, or custody&lt
Excellent understanding of an internal control environment and working knowledge of regulatory compliance within the financial services industry&lt
Experience within a regulated financial institution preferred&lt
Proven project management skills with the ability to juggle multiple responsibilities and deadlines&lt
Excellent analytical and problem solving skills, with high standards for quality, accuracy and attention to detail&lt
Excellent written and verbal communication skills&amp

&lt
Relevant Professional Certification (e.g., CIA, CPA, CFE, Series 7 etc.) or progress toward one&lt
Experience in financial services, fintech, payments, brokerage, custody, or crypto in an operations/compliance capacity&lt
Familiarity with regulatory expectations in the space&lt
Familiarity with audit methodology and standards (e.g., IIA Standards, COSO) and risk-based auditing&lt

Experience working in a fast paced organization&lt

column9: Coinbase is looking for an Internal Audit IT Associate Manager to join the Internal Audit team focusing primarily on global IT and security related audits. This role is based in the US (Remote) and will be reporting to an Internal Audit IT Senior Manager. The individual will be responsible for executing all aspects of audits, providing independent testing and audit coverage of technology, products and information security globally at Coinbase. This includes coverage over information and cyber security areas, infrastructure, platforms and applications, and governance over information technology and security. The individual has audit experience within one of the following areas: Information Security, IT governance, risk management or application security.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: As a part of a global internal audit team and working closely with the global IT audit portfolio, develop next generation audit practices to maintain a best-in-class global internal audit function.&lt
In depth understanding of local global regulatory requirements as it pertains to information technology, security and overall financial services applicable to Coinbase.&lt

Responsible for supporting Coinbase’s internal audit plan through the execution of information technology and security related audits from beginning to end. Key activities include audit planning, stakeholder management, fieldwork execution, reporting and validation of remediated audit findings.&lt
Develop audit reports on the effectiveness of the internal control structure for an executive level audience, ensuring reported findings are appropriately documented and supported by evidence.&lt
Develop customized and valuable solutions to issues identified during the course of audits performed.&lt
Manage and support internal audit staff and perform ad-hoc investigations/audits/reviews as required.&lt
Responsible for tracking and evaluating the adequacy and timeliness of management&#39
column4: s response and the corrective action taken on internal audit findings, including validation of remediation activities.&lt

column3: Remain current and increase knowledge of regulatory requirements that impact the organization, as well as in the areas of internal control, technology, security, business, company products and company information.&lt
Assist in the preparation of internal audit materials for the Audit Committee and Board of Directors.&lt
Validate the effectiveness of control function by leading periodic control certifications for all documented controls.&lt
Establish and maintain trusted relationships with business unit management across all levels.&lt
Work closely with both first and second lines of defense to maximize meetings utility, testing efficiencies and internal controls review coverage across the organization.&lt

Serve as a value-add and advisory function to business partners by identifying ways to address audit findings or opportunities for enhancements with the goal of mitigating risk and improving technology, security, and operational performance without compromising 3rd Line of Defense independence and objectivity.&lt
Assist with responses to regulatory inquiries as they relate to internal audit matters.&lt
Communicate with and educate process owners effectively, including non-finance individuals, on the importance of controls, an effective control environment, and the role of Internal Auditor.&lt
Promote new ideas / innovative means of executing projects and internal infrastructure enhancements focused on process or technology improvements.&amp
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&amp

column3: 6+ years of experience in internal audit with a heavy focus on technology, information technology and information security audit
column4: or experience in first line, operational, technology, software development, and security fields.&amp

column3: 1+ year of experience in an internal auditing manager position, managing processes and procedures&lt
Bachelor&#39
column4: s Degree in computer science/management information systems or related information technology or security fields and/or accounting or related fields.&lt

column3: Excellent understanding of cloud based technology stack, software and application development lifecycle, cloud configurations and security services, and overall an Enterprise technology stack operations, maintenance and oversight risks and controls.&amp
Proven project management skills with the ability to juggle multiple responsibilities and deadlines.&lt
Solid analytical and problem solving skills, with high standards for quality, accuracy and attention to detail.&lt
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.&lt
Self-driven, ability to work independently and in a team environment.&lt
Experience working in a fast paced organization.&lt
Excellent understanding of data analysis techniques.&lt
Demonstrated proficiency in Google Suite or MS Excel, Word and PowerPoint.&lt
&lt
Professional Internal Audit / IT / Security qualification (e.g., CISA, CFE, etc.) or comprehensive working knowledge of the Chartered Institute of Internal Auditor practice.&lt
Understanding of international regulatory requirements as it pertains to information technology and security.&lt
Understanding of crypto, payments and/or financial services industry and experience in auditing information systems and controls.&lt
Entrepreneurial attitude and experience with, or the ability to adapt to, a rapidly growing start-up with associated complexities and ambiguities.&lt
Experience working with/in an out-sourced or co-sourced internal audit function.&lt
Ability to work across functions and time zones.&lt

column9: The CX Compliance team is essential in ensuring that our global CX Compliance Operations function efficiently, comply with regulatory requirements, and consistently deliver high-quality resolutions. As a Compliance Lead Analyst, Compliance Operations within the CX Compliance team, you will be responsible for conducting end-to-end Transaction Monitoring investigations for customers (individuals/entities) that may lead to drafting and filing a SAR. Additionally, you will be proactively detecting emerging risks, optimizing compliance processes, and testing new tools and models before deployment to ensure smooth transition to production.&amp

column3: You will work independently and extensively with cross functional teams (such as Financial Crime Risk Investigations, Workforce Management, Content and Learning &amp
column5: Development stakeholders) prioritizing multiple tasks with minimal guidance, and remain open to feedback from all levels to drive continuous improvement in compliance operations.&amp

column3: The ideal candidate combines proven expertise in compliance operations production roles, analytical excellence, and the ability to think creatively to identify and propose impactful solutions.&lt
&amp
&lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Investigations &amp
Investigate and analyze manual referrals and automated alerts stemming from transaction monitoring systems&lt
Conduct due diligence research and KYC reviews on transaction activities across internal and external tools to assess legitimacy and mitigate risks.&lt
Document findings and create written narratives to support case investigations.&lt
Recommend case decisions based on investigative results in line with regulatory requirements and best practices&lt
Optimization &amp
Identify patterns or emerging risks&lt

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to evaluate, refine, and optimize compliance workflows, models, and procedures. Focus on improving efficiency, efficacy, scaling resources, and introducing industry best practices.&lt
Tool/Model Testing &amp
Act as a subject matter expert for new and existing Compliance Operations tools, processes, and future enhancements.&lt
Test compliance tools, rules, and/or detection models, providing actionable feedback to technology machine learning teams.&lt
Knowledge Leadership&lt
Remain current with industry standards and developments in the areas of KYC, BSA/AML, and OFAC, and apply regulatory requirements and internal policies and procedures to case investigation&lt
Maintain detailed documentation to demonstrate compliance with regulations and internal policies and procedures&amp
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Minimum of 3-5 years of relevant experience in traditional financial services, crypto, or the tech industry, with a focus on TMS/EDD compliance investigations, or regulatory matters.&lt
Strong interpersonal, analytical, and communication (verbal and written) skills&lt
Experience working with Google apps&lt
Flexible and adaptable to the evolving needs of a high-growth and fast paced organization environment&lt
Ability to think both strategically and operationally, with an eye for identifying risks and opportunities early.&lt
Creativity and problem-solving skills to address unstructured challenges.&lt
&lt
Relevant experience in EDD and Transaction Monitoring workflows including SAR narrative drafting and filing&lt
Experience in project management, data analytics, and/or third-party vendor management&lt
Proficiency with business intelligence tools such as Looker/Tableau/PowerBI, JIRA, Hummingbird, and Salesforce Service Cloud.&lt
Experience collaborating with internal external outsource business partners&lt
Professional certification in AML or Fraud (e.g. CAMS, CFCS, CFE)&amp
Advanced degree in business, finance, or customer experience (CX)&lt

column9: The Coinbase Compliance Testing and Assurance (“CTA”) function is an independent oversight function that is responsible for the planning and execution of testing and monitoring activities to assess whether the processes and controls supporting the compliance framework are designed and operating effectively. The Coinbase CTA team maintains the global policy and procedures for CTA activity enterprise wide, covering US legal entities and international entities. The CTA function collaborates with other members of the Enterprise Risk Management, Compliance, Legal, Internal Audit, and business functions in the execution of its mandate.&amp

column3: The CTA Manager will be responsible for supporting the CTA Senior Manager in the development, management, execution and continued improvement of the CTA program, with the focus on processes and controls for international entities, as well as enterprise-wide processes and controls for both US and international entities.&amp
&lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Assist in designing, implementing, and enhancing enterprise-wide CTA Program methodologies and governance documents.&lt
Participate in developing a risk-based annual CTA plan in collaboration with Compliance staff in the International, Institutional, Product, Tools and BSA/AML Teams.&amp
Contribute to the creation of periodic reporting for the annual CTA plan progress, results, and status of remediation plans.&lt
Perform and manage assigned CTA activities in the life cycle of planning, execution, reporting, and issue remediation, and provide escalation as needed.&amp
Prepare reports on results of CTA activities and work with stakeholders in root cause analysis and remediation plans.&lt
Maintain issues and remediation-related information and documentation in a central repository.&lt
Track and validate the implementation of remediation plans submitted by issue owners to verify that issues identified during testing and monitoring are remediated.&lt
Participate in training and/or awareness materials (as needed) regarding the CTA Program.&lt
Stay current on regulatory development and industry events to identify trends and emerging risks impacting Coinbase products and the CTA Program.&lt
Conduct other duties as assigned.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Advanced experience of executing CTA program, testing plan, and activities across a diverse range of financial services/products&lt
Working knowledge of laws, regulations, risk management practices for financial services&lt
Track record of delivering work outcome to budget and meet quality standard&lt
Hands-on involvement in developing and producing metrics and reporting&amp
Strong written/verbal communication, critical thinking and problem-solving skills&lt
Highly-motivated, analytical, organized, innovative and adaptive&lt
Good presentation skills, multi-tasking capability, team-oriented, and a self-starter&lt
Proven ability to work collaboratively with global partners in other functional units&lt
Flexibility to work extended hours to meet deadlines, when necessary&lt
Ability to work with limited direction from management when situations occur&lt
Experience in using data extraction and analysis tools (e.g. SQL, Tableau, SAS, Snowflake)&lt
Proficient in PowerPoint/Google Slides and Excel/Google Sheets&lt
A BA/BS degree in business, finance, and other related fields&lt
&lt
Experience in monitoring and testing third-party vendors&amp
Experience with and/or keen interest in cryptocurrency&lt
Experience working across multiple AML systems and programs simultaneously (e.g., experience in combinations of KYC, AML, and Sanctions)&lt
Experience with or previous exposure to financial services regulators&lt
Professional certifications (e.g. CRCM, CAMS, CIA, CISA, and CICA)&lt
&amp

column9: Coinbase is looking for a privacy subject matter expert to lead our Privacy operations. Oversight into DPIA’s and auditing Coinbase Inc.’s privacy program&lt

column5: to develop durable, scalable frameworks for addressing data-related incidents, regulatory inquiries, law enforcement demands, user information requests&lt

The Manager, Tax Accounting and Provision will lead our global quarterly and annual income tax provision process in accordance with ASC 740.&amp
column5: In this pivotal role, you will own the technical integrity of our financial tax reporting, managing SEC disclosures and providing the essential analysis to substantiate complex tax positions. column9: Coinbase is leading the expansion of digital currency into the financial mainstream. We are building a world-class legal team to support Coinbase and its affiliates as we continue to grow the company in the U.S. and internationally. You will be working in a cutting edge and fast paced environment, where disruptive technologies and unconventional thinking are the norm. Given the nascent state of the law, novel legal guidance is needed!&lt

column3: We are looking for a highly motivated, entrepreneurial, and experienced product counsel to join our product legal team as Counsel, Financial Services &amp
column5: Loyalty. You’ll be embedded with Coinbase product teams, and collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, UI/UX, and cross-department colleagues including privacy, regulatory and international counsel, Compliance and Finance. You’ll take a thought leadership role in advising our Product teams on specific aspects of product development and implementation.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Join and contribute to a best-in-class crypto product legal team, embedded with the Financial Services &amp
column5: Loyalty team within our Consumer business.&lt

column3: Integrate into the day-to-day operations of Coinbase Product, providing legal, business and strategic advice. Look around corners and creatively solve problems for the business as they plan, build, and execute on their product roadmap.&lt
Provide full product life-cycle counseling, from ideation and development to launch, operation and sunset.&lt
Ensure product compliance with applicable U.S. and international laws and regulations, in close collaboration with colleagues in Legal and the Compliance team.&lt
Identify issues that may fall outside your expertise, driving resolution by partnering with other members of the Coinbase legal team and with outside counsel.&lt
Work cross-departmentally, including with stakeholders such as Compliance, Communications, Finance, Tax, Security, Enterprise Risk Management, Data, Analytics, and Corporate Development.&lt
Respond promptly to a wide range of complex legal requests and coordinate closely with Legal department colleagues to ensure the swift delivery of smart, practical advice.&lt
Operate in a fast paced, demanding environment and pivot quickly to meet leadership&#39
Recognize priority initiatives and effectively manage your clients and your advice to scale your advice and provide the right level of guidance for request at hand.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Currently licensed in a U.S. state. J.D. or equivalent with 5+ years of legal experience, preferably with in-house experience with a consumer financial services business, a fintech or crypto/blockchain company.&lt

You have experience counseling product and engineering teams in connection with launching regulated financial / banking products and services in the fintech space, and have a clear understanding of the legal underpinnings supporting these products.&lt

You have a solid grasp of consumer financial services regulations and governmental agencies (Reg E, BSA, State MTLs, SEC, CFTC, FDIC, FinCEN, etc.), as well as familiarity with consumer services and internet regulations and agencies (UDAP/UDAAP, CDA 230, Privacy/GDPR)&lt
You are well-versed in Anti Money Laundering (AML)/Know-your-customer (KYC) requirements, and regulated e-money and money transmission obligations under banking laws.&lt
You have proven problem-solving, communication and interpersonal skills.&lt
You exercise excellent legal judgment with analytic rigor, prudential risk assessment, and a balance of realizable creativity and pragmatism.&lt
You are comfortable with risk-adjusted thinking, and can understand when interpretive flexibility is warranted.&lt

You are team-oriented and collaborative, you bring cross-departmental colleagues to the table, and build your practice to enable your clients to successfully build and launch crypto products at Coinbase.&lt

column9: We’re looking for a Product Manager to drive Coinbase’s work execution and collaboration platforms portfolio—including Jira, Linear, Asana, Slack, and emerging tools—and to lead the optimization of how work is planned, communicated, tracked, and delivered across Coinbase. You’ll define the strategy, roadmap, and governance for this ecosystem, ensuring our task and communication platforms operate as a cohesive, scalable system that balances team autonomy with organizational alignment, reduces context switching and noise, and improves both delivery transparency and focus time.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Own the strategy, roadmap, and governance for Coinbase’s collaborative work management (CWM) and collaboration platforms (Jira, Linear, Asana, Slack, etc.), ensuring they operate as a cohesive ecosystem that balances team autonomy with organizational alignment, reduces context switching, and improves execution speed and visibility.&lt

Lead structured discovery with Engineering, Product, Design, Operations, and other teams to identify workflow bottlenecks and collaboration friction. Define metrics and dashboards for velocity, delivery predictability, collaboration health, and documentation/search adoption, and use these insights to refine execution models and platform investments.&lt

Define and evolve an async‑first collaboration model that reduces low‑value chatter, improves signal‑to‑noise ratio, and connects conversations to the right work artifacts (tickets, docs, dashboards) across tools. Partner with enterprise search and knowledge platforms (e.g., Glean, Confluence/Docs, internal portals) to make “search before asking” the default behavior.&lt

Create and maintain a cross-platform center of excellence for work management and collaboration, with clear standards, paved paths, training, and governance across Jira, Linear, Asana, Slack, and related tools. Design “Day One” onboarding for Slack and work management that guides new employees to essential channels, projects, and playbooks, accelerating time‑to‑productivity.&lt

Lead change management and enablement for new tools, standards, and process changes, ensuring smooth transitions with minimal disruption and clear, opinionated documentation that helps teams adopt paved paths.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Proven experience owning or driving a portfolio of work management and/or collaboration platforms (e.g., Jira, Linear, Asana, Slack), including strategy, governance, and cross‑tool standards for different team contexts and methodologies.&lt
Track record of driving measurable improvements in team productivity, cycle time, delivery predictability, or collaboration health through changes to tools, workflows, and behaviors.&lt

Experience working with diverse personas (engineers, designers, product managers, operations, support) and high empathy for their day‑to‑day workflows, enabling you to design solutions that genuinely improve their experience.&lt
Demonstrated ability to influence without authority, driving adoption of new tools and practices across skeptical or change‑resistant teams and leaders.&lt
Data‑driven approach to decision making
column4: comfortable defining KPIs, building or partnering on dashboards, analyzing workflow and collaboration metrics, and presenting insights and tradeoffs to leadership.&lt

column3: Excellent product writing and stakeholder communication
column4: your documentation, PRDs, and guidance remove ambiguity, codify paved paths, and enable scalable self‑service.&lt

column3: &lt

Deep experience with Slack administration, APIs, workflow/automation, and app integrations—ideally including AI‑powered features (e.g., intelligent routing, summarization, task extraction, or predictive insights).&lt
Hands‑on experience with knowledge management and enterprise search platforms (e.g., Glean, Confluence, Notion) and shipping automations, integrations, and bots that measurably reduce toil.&lt
Track record of managing collaboration and work management tools at scale (5–10k+ users) across remote and globally distributed teams.&lt

column9: The Core Infrastructure team at Coinbase builds the foundational systems that keep our products online, secure, and scalable. We own the compute, networking, and storage platforms that power Coinbase applications globally. Our work ensures every product runs reliably, cost-effectively, and with the resiliency required in a highly regulated, always-on financial environment.&lt

column3: As an L5 Product Manager, you will own the roadmap for scalability, resilience, and efficiency of Coinbase’s core infrastructure. You will work closely with engineering and SRE teams to evaluate cloud platforms, improve compute and networking systems, and design resilient services that ensure Coinbase’s crypto applications run consistently, securely, and at global scale.&lt
&lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Own the product roadmap for Coinbase’s core infrastructure with emphasis on scalability, resiliency, and cost efficiency for global workloads.&lt
Collaborate with engineering, SRE, and infrastructure partners to design compute, networking, and storage systems that operate reliably across multiple regions and clouds.&lt
Lead evaluation and adoption of infrastructure technologies (compute, networking, storage, observability) to improve performance and maintain uptime during demand spikes.&lt
Anticipate growth driven by product launches, traffic surges, and global expansion, ensuring infrastructure capacity scales ahead of demand.&lt
Define and monitor SLAs for uptime, latency, and resiliency, and iterate with engineering to consistently meet or exceed these targets.&lt
Provide Coinbase teams with reliable, secure, and compliant infrastructure services that accelerate product delivery.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Ability to translate infrastructure challenges into clear product strategies and measurable outcomes.&lt
Track record of scaling globally distributed systems and core services to handle large, dynamic workloads with high reliability.&lt
Skilled collaborator with engineering, SRE, and security partners.&lt
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure technologies such as Kubernetes, service mesh, distributed storage, networking, and observability platforms.&lt
Comfortable balancing cost, performance, scalability, and compliance requirements in regulated industries.&lt
&lt
Experience with infrastructure for high-volume, low-latency, and always-on applications.&lt

column9: The DeFi Lend team drives Coinbase’s Lend product, offering customers a simple way to earn market-leading variable yield on their cash, powered under-the-hood by Coinbase Smart Contract Wallet and DeFi protocols like Morpho on Base. This role will bring the utility of crypto to everyday people by showcasing how crypto can make everyday financial services better. You will scale next-gen, onchain yield for millions of global customers, coordinating across teams across Coinbase&lt

column3: &lt

Own the end‑to‑end product area for DeFi Lend, from vision and strategy through execution, launch, and ongoing iteration across mobile and web, growing and scaling this new first-of-its-kind product&lt

Partner closely with Engineering and Design to design and ship non‑custodial flows that leverage Coinbase Smart Contract Wallets and Base, including deposit, withdrawal, rewards accrual, and claim experiences that abstract protocol complexity for users.&lt

Design product experiences that make DeFi risk and reward legible for everyday customers—clearly communicating smart contract, bad‑debt, and liquidity risks, as well as variable APYs, so users can make informed decisions..&lt

Drive integrated launch and rollout plans, including internal dogfooding, staged rollouts, global eligibility and blocklist strategy, and coordinated GTM across email, in‑app surfaces, and external channels.&lt

Ensure operational excellence post‑launch through robust observability, incident playbooks (e.g., for bad‑debt or liquidity events), and close coordination with our DeFi Borrow, Smart Wallet, and protocol partners.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you&lt

column3: 5+ years of Product Management experience, with a track record of shipping impactful consumer or prosumer financial products at scale.&lt
Experience working on complex financial, lending, or yield‑bearing products (TradFi or DeFi) where you’ve balanced customer experience, risk, and business performance.&lt

Strong comfort with technical systems and onchain concepts—you can partner credibly with engineers and protocol experts on topics like smart contracts, vaults, flow‑of‑funds, and L2 networks (e.g., Base).&lt

Demonstrated ability to define and drive multi‑quarter product strategy: setting a clear narrative, translating it into roadmaps, and delivering measurable results against growth and revenue goals.&lt
Analytical rigor and product judgment: you’re comfortable sizing opportunities (TAM, TVL, revenue), designing experiments, and interpreting protocol and user‑behavior data to inform decisions.&lt
Demonstrated passion for cryptocurrency, financial markets, and Coinbase’s mission to increase economic freedom in the world.&lt
&lt
Direct experience with DeFi lending protocols (e.g., Morpho, Aave, Compound) or vault‑based yield strategies, either as a builder or an active user.&lt
Experience building “crypto‑forward” consumer products that leverage onchain primitives (smart contract wallets, L2s, composable protocols) to deliver value that isn’t possible in TradFi.&lt
Familiarity with onchain activity such as interacting with Ethereum/Base addresses, using ENS, and engaging with dApps or DeFi services via self‑custodial wallets.&lt
Experience partnering with external protocol teams and risk‑management providers, and comfort navigating token‑based incentive programs and fee structures.&lt

column11: Activation team sits within the Consumer Growth organization and focuses on helping new users discover the core value of Coinbase as quickly and efficiently as possible, turning sign‑ups into activated, retained customers. We take a data‑driven, experimental approach to improving the user activation funnel from the moment a user lands on coinbase.com through signup, onboarding, and their first successful transaction.&amp

column3: As a Product Manager I on Growth Activation, you will help shape and execute the roadmap for these core activation experiences, working with engineering, design, and data to increase activation rates, reduce time‑to‑first‑transaction, and lay the foundation for long‑term engagement.&lt
&lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties)&lt

column3: Support the strategy and roadmap for the New User Experience, User Lifecycle, Multi-Product Discovery, and New User Incentives to help new users seamlessly complete KYC, add a payment method, and begin to invest on Coinbase.&lt
Analyze activation funnels and dashboards to identify where new users drop off, size opportunities, and propose clear, experiment‑backed solutions.&lt
Write simple, clear product requirements and user stories, and partner with Engineering and Design to ship improvements to NUX flows, incentives, and related UX.&lt
Run A/B tests across the entire lifecycle of a user’s first 30 days – from onboarding to incentives – in partnership with Data Science and experimentation teams

Collaborate with adjacent teams (Identity, Onboarding, Payments, Incentives, Growth Foundations) to ensure activation experiences are technically sound, cohesive, high quality, and consistent across platforms.&lt

Contribute to on‑call and operational excellence for Activation‑owned services (e.g., new user incentives, notifications, cross-product discovery), working with engineering partners to keep critical new‑user paths reliable.&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: 3+ years of product management or closely related experience (e.g., growth, operations, program management, or analytics) in consumer products.&lt
Experience working on user funnels (signup, onboarding, or checkout), including using data to understand drop‑offs and measure impact of product changes.&lt
Comfort with experimentation and basic analytics (e.g., setting success metrics, reading A/B test results, partnering with Data Science or Analytics).&lt
Strong collaboration skills
column4: you work well with Engineering, Design, and cross‑functional partners and can drive alignment on priorities and tradeoffs.&lt

column3: Clear written and verbal communication
column4: able to turn ambiguous problems into simple problem statements, PRDs, and next steps.&lt

column3: Interest in crypto and financial products, with curiosity to learn quickly about our users and ecosystem.&lt
&lt
Prior experience in growth, activation, onboarding, or lifecycle roles focused on acquisition‑to‑activation or early retention.&lt
Familiarity with experimentation and lifecycle tooling (e.g., in‑app experimentation platforms, A/B testing tools, or CRM/notification systems).&lt
Experience working with incentives, referrals, or gamification programs for new users.&lt
Hands‑on experience as a crypto user (buying, earning, or on‑chain activity).&lt

Every time someone sends crypto from one exchange to another, global regulations require that customer information travels with the transaction — similar to how the banking system works for wire transfers. This is called the Travel Rule, and it&#39
column4: s one of the most important regulatory frameworks shaping the future of crypto payments.&lt

column3: Coinbase built and operates TRUST (Travel Rule Universal Solution Technology), the industry&#39
column4: s leading solution for Travel Rule compliance, with 100+ member exchanges and Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs) in the network. Think of it as the SWIFT of crypto identity exchange — and we&#39
column5: re working to make it the global standard.&lt

column3: As the PM for this space, you&#39
column4: ll own both the platform (APIs, backend infrastructure, TRUST network) and the consumer-facing experience — making sure that what could feel like regulatory friction instead feels seamless, trustworthy, and fast for end users. You&#39
column5: ll partner closely with the Institutional product group and other teams across Coinbase who integrate with your platform, and you&#39
column6: ll drive the business growth of TRUST as a network competing against players like Notabene and Sumsub.&lt

column3: This is a unique role that blends consumer product craft, platform and API thinking, and business development — all in a high-stakes regulatory domain with global scope.&lt

column13: is looking for a Product Manager to play a pivotal role in helping our users understand and manage their crypto taxes, portfolio performance, and realized / unrealized gains and losses across their Coinbase activity and beyond. You will lead the ideation, development, and execution of a best‑in‑class retail tax experience and tax center that simplifies an incredibly complex problem space—multiple wallets, on‑chain transfers, gas fees, and incomplete cost basis—while ensuring Coinbase can meet its tax reporting obligations in key markets. You will also own core parts of the consumer trading experience visited by millions of users every month, including transaction history and performance. You will leverage your understanding of financial transactions, tax concepts, and trading performance to build engaging, trustworthy experiences that help users manage their transactions, P&amp
column15: L, and tax obligations in one place.&lt

column3: &lt
&lt
column5: Own the Retail Tax, Transactions, and Performance roadmap&lt
column7: : Understand user behaviors, emerging tax regulations, and market trends to conceptualize and implement a roadmap that makes it dramatically easier for customers to view their transactions, track their performance, and handle their crypto taxes (including handling use cases like multiple wallets, on‑chain transfers, and gas fees).&lt

column3: &lt
column7: : Define success metrics and run experiments to evaluate the effectiveness of tax and performance experiences (e.g., completeness of tax profiles, reduction in missing cost basis, engagement with tax tools, downstream filing behavior). Use data and user feedback to continuously iterate and improve.&lt

column3: &lt
column7: : Own product development from conception to launch and beyond, iterating until you’ve created the most seamless, trusted retail tax and performance experience in crypto.&lt

column3: &lt
column7: : Ensure the Retail Tax and performance surfaces are reliable, performant, and intuitive during critical periods such as tax season and major market events.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: Align teams on a shared vision&lt
column7: : Drive clarity and alignment across Consumer Trade and partner teams on the Retail Tax and performance vision, requirements, and execution plan, and keep everyone focused on the highest‑impact problems.&lt

Domain expertise in financial transactions and taxes, such as experience with tax reporting products, cost basis, gains/loss calculations, or evaluating trading performance.&lt
Strong analytical and data skills, with experience working on P&amp
column5: L, performance analytics, or similar financial reporting surfaces for retail users.&lt

column3: Consumer UX experience and a strong product/design sense for presenting complex financial information in simple, understandable, and engaging ways.&lt
Experience building 0→1 products in high‑ambiguity spaces, creatively solving problems under constraints while maintaining a high quality bar.&lt
Ability to work with complex stakeholder sets, including Tax, Legal, Compliance, Finance, support, and external partners, and to balance regulatory, business, and user needs.&lt

Crypto literacy and hands‑on exposure to crypto products—ideally including on‑chain activity such as DEX trading or send/receive flows—so you can deeply understand the pain points of tax in a multi‑wallet, multi‑chain world.&lt
Excellent communication and influence skills, with a track record of shaping strategic decisions and driving alignment across senior stakeholders.&lt
An entrepreneurial mindset and ability to succeed in a fast‑paced, evolving environment.&lt
Aptitude for learning and the ability to quickly ramp on new and complex regulatory and technical domains.&lt
&lt
Direct experience with US tax reporting rules for digital assets (e.g., cost basis, 1099 reporting, wash sales, staking rewards).&lt

This role sits at the center of Coinbase Institutional’s Prime &amp
column5: Markets strategy. You will own how our most complex products show up to institutional clients globally, from spot and derivatives to cross-margin, liquidity, and execution workflows.&lt

column3: Markets refers to Coinbase’s institutional exchange, derivatives, and execution stack accessed via Prime, including spot, futures, options, liquidity access, and market structure capabilities.&lt

You’ll partner closely with Product, Markets, Sales, Legal, and Comms to translate deeply technical capabilities into clear narratives, confident sales motion, and measurable client adoption across regions with different regulatory constraints. This is a product-first, revenue-adjacent PMM role with real ownership and influence.&lt
You will execute GTMs today while helping define how Coinbase Institutional goes to market in 2026 and beyond.&lt
column7: You will support products used by hundreds of institutional clients globally, including hedge funds, asset managers, and market makers executing at scale across spot and derivatives markets.&lt

column3: &lt
&lt
Define and evolve the positioning for Prime and Markets as a single institutional operating platform.&lt
Lead messaging across spot, futures, options, cross-margin, execution, and liquidity access.&lt
Pressure-test narratives with Sales, Product, Legal, and real clients to ensure credibility at hedge fund scale and regulatory durability across regions.&lt
&lt
Act as PMM DRI for major Prime and Markets launches, including derivatives and cross-margin initiatives.&lt
Build GTM strategies that span product readiness, sales enablement, client communications, and events.&lt
Own sequencing and clarity around what’s live, what’s next, and what action clients should take.&lt
&lt
Create high-signal enablement materials: talk tracks, FAQs, battlecards, demos, and client-ready narratives.&lt
Partner tightly with Sales, STPs, and Coverage to reflect real objections, real deals, and buying behavior.&lt
Reduce complexity for the field by delivering fewer, sharper, higher-impact assets.&lt
&lt
Translate between Product, Markets, Prime, Sales, and Leadership.&lt
Turn complex technical detail into institutional-grade clarity without oversimplifying.&lt
Influence roadmap and prioritization conversations through market-backed insight.&lt
&lt
column5: Raise the Bar for Institutional PMM&lt

column3: Establish best practices for technical PMM work across the Institutional org.&lt
Shape how GTMs, product showcases, and launches are run going forward.&lt
Mentor junior PMMs and contribute to a stronger, more rigorous PMM function.&lt
&lt
column5: What We Look For In You:&amp

column3: &lt
7+ years in product marketing or adjacent roles (PMM, product strategy, institutional GTM).&lt
Meaningful experience with institutional trading, derivatives, or market infrastructure.&lt
&lt
Strong understanding of derivatives markets, including futures, options, margining, and execution models.&lt
Comfort discussing liquidity, capital efficiency, risk, and market structure with institutional clients.&lt
You speak the language of sophisticated trading firms and asset managers.&lt
&lt
Experience building narratives and GTMs that work across regions with different regulatory constraints.&lt
Ability to balance global consistency with local compliance and market realities.&lt
&lt
Ability to simplify complex systems into clear, compelling positioning.&lt
Strong instincts around prioritization, sequencing, and tradeoffs.&lt
Confidence to push back when narratives don’t hold up.&lt
&lt
Thrive in ambiguity and take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks.&lt
Build structure where none exists and move work forward without waiting for direction.&lt
Balance speed with rigor.&lt
&lt
Proven ability to partner with Product, Sales, Markets, Legal, and Comms.&lt
Comfortable leading without authority and aligning strong stakeholders.&lt
Trusted partner because you are prepared, direct, and knowledgeable.&amp
&lt
Exceptional written and verbal communication.&lt
column5: Ability to brief leadership, influence decisions, and represent product externally.&lt

column3: Bring POV, not just summaries.&lt
&lt
Experience in prime brokerage, derivatives platforms, electronic trading, or market infrastructure.&lt
Background in TradFi, crypto-native firms, or both.&lt
Prior experience supporting institutional sales teams.&lt
Familiarity with APIs, trading UIs, or execution workflows.&lt

column9: Prediction markets are one of the fastest growing areas in trading– and it’s no wonder, because they offer a way for traders to put money behind their beliefs in sports, politics, and culture. They can be powerful truth signals since traders have financial motivation to be right and there is no third party setting the odds with a built-in edge.&lt

column3: Coinbase launched prediction markets early this year and you’ll be supporting the team in growing awareness and adoption.&lt
&lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Be the voice of the customer: immerse yourself in the prediction markets ecosystem and understand which segments of users are trading which types of prediction markets across sports, politics/economics, culture, and more.&lt

Develop growth strategies and tactics: ideate, refine, and execute growth strategies for ongoing news and big events (e.g. sports tournaments, elections). You’ll be using channels and tactics like social, email/push, influencers, paid ads, events, and incentives&lt
Lead product and feature launches: put together go to market strategy for feature launches like parlays&lt
Measure and report against the performance of product marketing initiatives&lt
Think big about the future of prediction markets and how we can use them to better predict outcomes and make the world aware of these predictions&lt
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: Minimum of 5 years of marketing experience within the trading/crypto space or the FinTech space&lt
Demonstrated track record of ideating and implementing growth strategies on products&lt
Demonstrated track record of creating customer segmentation and insights&lt
Powerful storyteller: written, verbal, and visual&lt
Advanced quantitative and deductive skills. Ability to distinguish between the signal and the noise and make data-guided actions&lt
Outstanding project management skills&lt
Self-starter and ability to be ready to roll-up your sleeves and get things done&lt
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across multiple teams&lt

column9: Coinbase Legal Operations is looking for aProgram Manager, Contracts &amp
column11: Analytics to join our team focused on the hands-on configuration and ongoing optimization of our Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) systems. The primary mission is to provide deep technical and operational support to the Institutional Sales team, ensuring seamless, compliant, and highly efficient client contracting processes. You will be the key driver in building, maintaining, and integrating CLM technologies like Ironclad, Evisort, and Salesforce, as well as developing and managing the comprehensive contracts reporting framework and system of record for all agreements.&lt

column3: &lt
column5: What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):&lt

column3: Build and maintain contracting workflows within Ironclad, with a focus on accelerating the Institutional Sales contract pipeline.&lt
Lead project management of large, cross-functional contracts-focused initiatives, ensuring delivery on time and within scope.&lt

Support integration planning and execution between CLM systems (e.g., Ironclad, Evisort) and other core business systems (e.g., Salesforce), specifically to streamline the institutional contracting process.&lt
Develop and manage a comprehensive contracts reporting framework, including data extraction, metadata integrity, and building data models to maintain the system of record for all agreements.&lt
Partner with Institutional Sales to surface contract insights and analytics from their client agreements.&amp
&lt
column5: What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):&lt

column3: 5 - 7+ years proven program or project management experience, preferably within Legal Operations or a related field.&lt
Direct experience supporting a fast-paced Institutional Sales or similar B2B client-facing team, with a deep understanding of their contracting needs and priorities.&lt
Expert-level hands-on experience with Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) systems, specifically Ironclad.&lt
Strong working knowledge of legal technology, including AI tooling for contract review and data extraction and analytics (e.g., Evisort).&lt
Ability to communicate technical concepts to a non-technical audience and translate business needs into technical requirements.&lt
Demonstrated experience with data management, reporting, and building data models for contracts or other structured business data.&lt

Demonstrates the ability to responsibly use generative AI tools and copilots (e.g., LibreChat, Gemini, Glean) in daily workflows, continuously learn as tools evolve, and apply human-in-the-loop practices to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, and quality.&lt
&lt
Experience with integration planning and implementation for core business systems like Salesforce.&lt
Experience in the financial services or technology industry, particularly with legal/compliance requirements related to institutional clients.&lt
Familiarity with the legal, regulatory, and technical landscape of cryptocurrency and Web3.&lt
Familiarity with building dashboards, program governance and creating more efficient processes related to intake and workflow management.&lt
